page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register weak to poor results. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core, stress-ng multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also perform in the average range, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

Given the high memory density relative to compute resources, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints without demanding high single-threaded CPU performance. It is suited for database operations, as evidenced by average Passmark database and Redis SET performance, as well as multi-threaded software compilation and general-purpose memory-bound applications. However, it is less suitable for intensive single-threaded processing, high-throughput static web serving, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates a performance tradeoff: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, rank in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core operations, including Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, consistently reach average tiers. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads like database operations and software compilation, while remaining less optimal for single-threaded tasks or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores at 2.5 GHz.
96.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU.
Average multi-core performance contrasted with weak single-core execution.
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Suited for database operations, Redis, and multi-threaded compilation.
